code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a written work into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to produce a clause for each predicate
    def clause(triple: Triple) -> str:
        s, p, o = triple.subject, triple.predicate, triple.object
        # Basic patterns for common predicates
        patterns = {
            "author": f"{s} was written by {o}",
            "isbnNumber": f"It has ISBN {o}",
            "followedBy": f"It is followed by {o}",
            "precededBy": f"It was preceded by {o}",
            "language": f"It is written in {o}",
            "mediaType": f"It is available as {o}",
            "numberOfPages": f"It contains {o} pages",
            "country": f"It was published in {o}",
            "almaMater": f"The author studied at {o}",
            "doctoralAdvisor": f"The author was advised by {o}",
            "nationality": f"The author is {o}",
            "residence": f"The author lives in {o}",
            "codenCode": f"It carries the CODEN {o}",
            "issnNumber": f"It has ISSN {o}",
            "LCCN_number": f"It has LCCN {o}",
            "abbreviation": f"It is abbreviated as {o}",
            "academicDiscipline": f"It belongs to the discipline of {o}",
            "publisher": f"It was published by {o}",
            "firstPublicationYear": f"It was first published in {o}",
            "editor": f"It was edited by {o}",
            "impactFactor": f"It has an impact factor of {o}",
            "oclcNumber": f"It has OCLC number {o}",
            "libraryofCongressClassification": f"It is classified under {o}",
            "genre": f"It is a {o} work",
            "literaryGenre": f"It is a {o} work",
            "eissnNumber": f"It has e‑ISSN {o}",
            "frequency": f"It is issued {o}",
            "headquarter": f"The organization is headquartered in {o}",
            "leader": f"The leader is {o}",
            "birthPlace": f"The author was born in {o}",
            "affiliation": f"It is affiliated with {o}",
            "city": f"It is located in {o}",
            "president": f"The president is {o}",
            "state": f"It is in the state of {o}",
            "spokenIn": f"It is spoken in {o}",
            "influencedBy": f"It was influenced by {o}",
            "family": f"It belongs to the {o} family",
            "ethnicGroup": f"It is associated with the {o} ethnic group",
            "largestCity": f"The largest city is {o}",
            "location": f"It is located in {o}",
            "deathPlace": f"The author died in {o}",
            "notableWork": f"Notable work includes {o}",
            "parentCompany": f"It is owned by {o}",
            "regionServed": f"It serves the region of {o}",
            "founder": f"It was founded by {o}",
            "capital": f"The capital is {o}",
            "demonym": f"The demonym is {o}",
            "leaderTitle": f"The leader holds the title of {o}",
            "nickname": f"It is nicknamed {o}",
            "genus": f"It belongs to the genus {o}",
            "releaseDate": f"It was released on {o}",
            "birthDate": f"The author was born on {o}",
        }
        return patterns.get(p, f"{s} {p} {o}")

    # Build clauses, starting with a clause that mentions the main subject
    main_subject = triples[0].subject
    main_clauses = [clause(triple) for triple in triples if triple.subject == main_subject]

    # If there are other subjects, add them as additional information
    other_clauses = [clause(triple) for triple in triples if triple.subject != main_subject]

    all_clauses = main_clauses + other_clauses

    # Join clauses into a fluent sentence
    if len(all_clauses) == 1:
        sentence = all_clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(all_clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + all_clauses[-1] + "."

    # Capitalize first letter
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its BLUE score and an inspiration program and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: WrittenWork. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:

Predicate: author - Example triple: (1634: The Baltic War | author | David Weber)
